page: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by the arm64-based AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 192.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU execution, Geekbench multi-core metrics, and Passmark CPU physics tests ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top 10%, with cached read bandwidth reaching 11,096.49 GB/sec and uncached read bandwidth at 481.1 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderately lower, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25% and middle 50% respectively.

The server demonstrates high efficiency for parallelized, compute-heavy workloads. Top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations make it suitable for CI/CD pipelines, in-memory caching, and database operations. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. The 2.0 GB of RAM per core ratio represents a tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density over memory-intensive tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server utilizing the arm64 AWS Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory. This configuration yields a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the c8g.metal-24xl achieves top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Conversely, single-core metrics and text processing fall into the average tier. This server is optimized for highly parallelized workloads such as software compilation, Redis caching, and database operations, offering high compute density while trading off local storage availability and high per-core memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 arm64 Neoverse-V2 cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.metal-24xl is an arm64 bare-metal server with 96 dedicated cores, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: An arm64 bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated cores and DDR5 memory, optimized for highly parallelized compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated arm64 AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Optimized for software compilation, Redis caching, and parallel computing
